opener.dateline:
Legation of the United States,
London, November 26, 1892. (Received December 6.)

div.p: Sir: Referring to my dispatch numbered 841, of the 21st instant, I have the honor to inclose herewith a copy of a note which I have received from the Earl of Rosebery, in reply to the communication which I addressed to his lordship on the 21st ultimo, relative to the conduct of Capt. Davis, of H. M. S. Royalist, to our commercial agent at Butaritari, Gilbert Islands.

frus:attachment.head: The Earl of Rosebery to Mr. White.
frus:attachment.p: Sir: It is with sincere regret that I learn from your note of the 21st instant, that the proclamation of a British protectorate over the Gilbert Islands was accompanied by an incident to which your Government considers itself compelled to call attention.

The matter shall receive my immediate and careful consideration. In the meantime, I would beg you to convey to the United States Government an assurance that the rights and interests of United States citizens established in the Gilbert Islands will be fully recognized and respected by the British authorities.
